1.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 3.10.9 is the lake Gennesareth naval slaughter; 'caught by the vessels' verbatim. Same body of water as Luke 5, same men-caught-from-water image.

2.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 3.10.10 shows Vespasian on his tribunal at Taricheae deliberating clemency, then letting captives depart by one road into a trap. Rise-and-walk correspondence is satiric-interpretive.

3.verification: VERIFIED (corrected by the maintainer): Wars 4.2.2 — Titus offers the security of the Romans' 'right hands' three times, adjacent to John's Sabbath plea in 4.2.3. Luke 6 pairs Sabbath + withered right hand; same pairing, adjacent, in both texts.

4.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.5.2 — death of Ananus, high priests slain and cast out unburied; 'beginning of the destruction' verbatim. Cast-out correspondence is verb-level.

5.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.7.1 depicts John of Gischala as tyrannizing deceiver — name + villainy real; no demon language in Josephus.

6.verification: VERIFIED: 'too small for an army, too many for a gang of thieves' faithful; Gadara enters at 4.7.3 immediately after. Legion/demons framing interpretive; place-name + sequence real.

7.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.7.4 — the fugitives from Gadara stream into the larger mass which Placidus's cavalry drives; transfer-into-larger-body present, at Gadara, in sequence.

8.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.7.4-5 — the multitude driven violently by horsemen like a herd.

9.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.7.5-6 — fugitives pushed into the Jordan; 'Jordan could not be passed over by reason of the dead bodies.' Driven-herd-drowned imagery matches Luke 8:33, at Gadara.

10.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 4.10.7 — Vespasian concludes Divine Providence gave him the empire (verbatim) and recalls Josephus's prophecy; a divine-identification scene at Peter's-confession position.

11.verification: VERIFIED: Titus orders Josephus's chain CUT rather than loosed (Wars 4.10.7, verbatim); but binding/loosing is Matt 16:19, imported into Luke's sequence position.

12.verification: VERIFIED: three-factions text verbatim (Wars 5.1.4); not-following-with-us correspondence interpretive.

13.verification: VERIFIED: Luke 9:51-52 sets face toward Jerusalem, sends messengers ahead; Titus orders forces to meet him at Jerusalem and marches (Wars 5.1.6, verbatim). Same destination, same advance-party structure, same pivot.

14.verification: VERIFIED: quote faithful (Wars 5.1.6); no-burial/no-looking-back correspondence loose — the army 'remembering what they had formerly suffered' reads as an opposite.

15.verification: VERIFIED: Titus marches through Samaria on the Jerusalem road (Wars 5.2.1); Samaritan-kindness correspondence interpretive; the parable is set Jerusalem-Jericho.

16.verification: VERIFIED: legion joins Caesar AT NIGHT near the visible city (Wars 5.2.3, verbatim); three-loaves/three-legions equation is the satiric layer.

17.verification: VERIFIED: factions 'dashing one against another perpetually' in the doomed city (Wars 5.2.4); divided-house-unto-desolation is literally the content of both texts, and Josephus's own thesis.

18.verification: VERIFIED: the seditious lament sitting armed and idle within their walls while a stronger power builds against them (Wars 5.2.4 speech); armed-man-overcome-by-a-stronger structure in both texts, adjacent to #17 as in Luke.

19.verification: VERIFIED: 'Jews became still more and more in number' faithful; crowds-gathering correspondence generic.

20.verification: VERIFIED: Caesar: Jews 'contrive stratagems and lay ambushes' (Wars 5.3.4); Luke's lying-in-wait is verbal — idiom-level match.

21.verification: VERIFIED: Jesus ben Ananias (Wars 6.5.3) — a Jesus crying woes over Jerusalem seven years, silent under scourging, killed by a siege stone. Mainstream passion-narrative comparandum. Sequence caveat: reaches ahead to Wars 6.

22.verification: VERIFIED: people 'who had no concern in the sedition' suffer indiscriminately (Wars 5.3.1); graded-stripes logic absent.

23.verification: VERIFIED: 'sedition, which had been divided into three factions, was now reduced to two' (Wars 5.3.1, verbatim) vs Luke's 'three against two' — the 3/2 numbers are literally in both texts.

24.verification: VERIFIED: Titus 'cut down all the fruit trees' before Jerusalem (Wars 5.3.2). Verbatim lexical overlap with Luke 13:6-9; sequence position consistent.

25.verification: VERIFIED (±1 re-read): Wars 5.3.3 gate stratagem — men outside the wall 'pretended that they were excluded by force, and petitioned those that were within to let them in'; others falsely promised 'to open the gates'. Luke 13:25's shut door and knocking has a direct counterpart two sections before the citation.

26.verification: VERIFIED: Titus circles the wall deliberating where to build siege works (Wars 5.6.2); Atwill's 'build a tower' quote is a paraphrase — the one loose citation found.

27.verification: VERIFIED: Josephus sent to discourse terms of peace to those on the wall (Wars 5.6.2) vs Luke's king sending an ambassage desiring conditions of peace; paired with the tower saying in both texts.

28.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 5.6.3 — watchmen cry 'THE SON COMETH' as the white siege stone flies; Romans blacken the stones (hidden from eyes). Crying stones + stones hidden from eyes textually present.

29.verification: VERIFIED: Titus's circumvallation wall (Wars 5.12.1-2) vs Luke 19:43 trench/compass-round. Mainstream scholarship reads the same correspondence as Luke knowing the siege. Sequence caveat: 5.12 follows the #30 material.

30.verification: VERIFIED (±1 re-read): 'the multitude of the robbers were thrust out of the inner court of the temple' (Wars 6.5.1); KJV 'thieves' = Whiston 'robbers' (lestai). Sequence caveat: sits at 6.5.1, not the cited 5.7.2 position.

31.verification: VERIFIED: the Daily Sacrifice failed on 17 Panemus with the desolation oracle invoked (Wars 6.2.1) — precisely Daniel's abomination trigger carried by Luke 21/Matt 24. Note the abomination wording itself is Matthew's.

32.verification: VERIFIED: Wars 6.3.4 — Mary of Bethezob ('house of Hyssop'), daughter of Eleazar, slays, roasts, and eats half her son during Passover-season famine; 'portentous to posterity'. All cited elements textually present.

33.verification: VERIFIED: Life 75 [420-421] — Josephus (bar Matthias) has three crucified acquaintances taken down; two die, one survives. Elements present; Arimathea/Bar-Matthias name-play interpretive. From Life, outside the Wars sequence.

34.verification: VERIFIED (endpoints corrected): John 21 pairs Simon Peter's foretold death ('carry thee whither thou wouldest not') with John spared ('if I will that he tarry till I come'); Josephus pairs Simon bar Gioras executed at the triumph (Wars 7.5.6, rope on his head) with John of Gischala spared to perpetual imprisonment (Wars 6.9.4). A Simon condemned and a John spared, literally in both texts; identifying the Gospel pair with the rebel leaders is the interpretive layer. Previously mis-anchored to the Nazareth brow-of-the-hill decode (Luke 4 / Wars 3.7), which chapter 5's coda interleaves.
